The creation of an organ of the press for political, economic, cultural, sport, social, recreative or scientific information is free and is not subject to prior authorization. The regime of the press
All the citizens have the right to freely constitute associations, [and] economic, cultural and social groups as well as societies, under reserve of conforming to the formalities specified [édictées]

The secrecy of correspondence [and] of postal, telegraphic, telephonic and electronic communications[,] is inviolable. Restriction of this inviolability, may only be ordered in application of the law.

All the citizens of the Republic have the right to move themselves and to establish themselves freely on the whole extent of the national territory as well as abroad. These freedoms are exercised with

The right of property [propriété] is guaranteed by this Constitution. It can only be infringed in the case of public necessity legally established [constatée], under reserve of a just and prior indemn

The domicile is inviolable. [A] search may only be ordered by the judge or by the other authorities designated by the law. Searches may only be executed in the forms prescribed by them. The measures i

Marriage and the family constitute the natural and moral base of the human community. They are placed under the protection of the State. The State and the public collectivities have the duty to see to
Forced marriage is a violation of individual liberty. It is forbidden and punished within the conditions established by the law.
The woman has the right of having her own patrimony[,] as [does] her husband. She has the right to the personal management of her assets.
The Capital of the Republic of Senegal is Dakar. It may be transferred to any other place on the national territory.

The State and the public collectivities create the preliminary conditions and the public institutions which guarantee the education of children.

The President of the Republic is elected by direct universal suffrage and by the absolute majority of the suffrage expressed.
The duration of the mandate of the President of the Republic is of five years. No one may exercise more than two consecutive mandates.

During the duration of the replacement, the provisions of Articles 49, 51, 86, 87 and 103 are not applicable.

The President of the Republic is the guardian of the Constitution. He is the premier Protector of the Arts and Letters of Senegal. He incarnates the national unity. He is the guarantor of the regular

The President of the Republic signs the ordinances and the decrees. The acts of the President of the Republic, with the exception of those which he accomplishes by virtue of Articles 26 paragraphs 2 t
The President of the Republic appoints to the civil offices [emplois].

The President of the Republic is responsible for the National Defense. He presides over the Superior Council of the National Defense and the National Council of Security. He is the Supreme Head [Chef]
The President of the Republic accredits the ambassadors and the extraordinary envoys before foreign powers. The ambassadors and extraordinary envoys of the foreign powers are accredited to him.
The President of the Republic has the right of pardon.
The President of the Republic can address messages to the Nation.

The Government is composed of the Prime Minister, Head of the Government, and the Ministers. The Government conducts and coordinates the policy of the Nation under the direction of the Prime Minister.

The President of the Republic communicates with the National Assembly by messages which he delivers [prononce] or which he has read and which do not give rise to any debate.

The judicial power is the guardian of the rights and freedoms defined by the Constitution and the law.
